response.justification: Bogdan has a broad and accomplished web/full-stack background with genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective and the Green Map GIS platform, but falls significantly short of the core requirements for this role. C++ experience is isolated to a single position at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project; no Rust is mentioned. The geospatial work is focused on data collection and visualization rather than routing tile pipelines or navigation systems. His primary and recent expertise — TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB — is entirely misaligned with the C++/Linux systems engineering this position demands.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective open-source platform and Green Map GIS application, demonstrating familiarity with geo-data concepts
High-performance, low-latency distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and real-time metadata
Demonstrated ability to operate across the full stack and adopt new technologies, with evidence of embedded, infrastructure, and systems-level work
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and entirely outdated — last used professionally in 2014–2015; no Rust experience mentioned anywhere in the resume
Zero experience with routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or real-time traffic signal integration
Current and recent technical focus is TypeScript/Node.js/React, which is orthogonal to the C++/Linux systems engineering core of this role
